# UC-22.3.27 · DORA TLPT — Findings Severity, Remediation Owner, and Due Date Tracking

## Description

TLPT produces material findings. Monitoring remediation SLAs by severity with linkage to ICT third-party providers evidences closed-loop treatment required for resilience testing programs.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes tlpt:finding. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map severity to internal SLAs; (2) auto-create problems in ITSM; (3) escalate critical past-due to management body; (4) join provider_id to RoI; (5) quarterly export for regulator file. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=tlpt:finding`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Tenable/attack platform JSON.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="tlpt:finding"` (finding_id, engagement_id, severity, owner, due_date, status, related_provider_id).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map severity to internal SLAs; (2) auto-create problems in ITSM; (3) escalate critical past-due to management body; (4) join provider_id to RoI; (5) quarterly export for regulator file.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:finding" status!="closed" earliest=-400d
| eval due=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where due<now() OR isnull(owner)
| stats count by severity, related_provider_id, owner
| sort severity, - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA TLPT — Findings Severity, Remediation Owner, and Due Date Tracking** — TLPT produces material findings. Monitoring remediation SLAs by severity with linkage to ICT third-party providers evidences closed-loop treatment required for resilience testing programs.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="tlpt:finding"` (finding_id, engagement_id, severity, owner, due_date, status, related_provider_id). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Tenable/attack platform JSON. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: tlpt:finding. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="tlpt:finding", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where due<now() OR isnull(owner)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by severity, related_provider_id, owner** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue), Column chart by severity, Single value (open critical).

## SPL

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:finding" status!="closed" earliest=-400d
| eval due=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where due<now() OR isnull(owner)
| stats count by severity, related_provider_id, owner
| sort severity, - count
```

## Visualization

Table (overdue), Column chart by severity, Single value (open critical).
